cau_units.title: Download and Registration
test_steps: The user should be able to download the mobile application free of charge through an application store and subsequently complete the registration process by providing a user name, password, and e-mail address. The user has a compatible mobile handset with an active Internet connection and access to a supported application store.

Open the application store on the mobile device. 2. Search for and locate the Amazing Lunch Indicator application. 3. Download and install the application at no cost. 4. Launch the application. 5. Select 'Not registered? Click here' on the login page. 6. Provide the required registration fields and submit.
cau_units.expected_result: The application is available for free download. The registration form accepts the provided information and confirms account creation.
cau_units.actual_result: The application was successfully downloaded at no cost. Registration was completed without errors by all testers.
cau_units.tester_observations: No issues were reported. Testers found the registration flow straightforward.

cau_units.title: User Log-In and Automatic Login
cau_units.description: A registered user should be able to log in to the mobile application. Login credentials should be stored on the device so that subsequent launches do not require re-entry of credentials.
preconditions: The user has successfully registered (FR3 passed).
test_steps: Launch the application and log in with valid credentials. 2. Close the application completely. 3. Relaunch the application and observe behaviour.
cau_units.expected_result: Login succeeds on the first attempt. On relaunch, the user is taken directly to the search page without being prompted to log in again.
cau_units.actual_result: Login succeeded for all testers. Automatic login on relaunch functioned correctly on all tested handsets.
cau_units.tester_observations: Testers appreciated the automatic login feature and noted it significantly reduced friction for repeat use.

linked_crus.action: Given that a user has registered, then the user should be able to log in to the mobile application.
The log-in information will be stored on the phone and in the future the user should be logged in automatically.

cau_units.title: Search Functionality
cau_units.description: A logged-in user should be able to search for restaurants using one or more of the available search options: price, destination, restaurant type, specific dish, and free-text search.
preconditions: The user is logged in and the device has an active Internet and GPS connection.
test_steps: From the search page, select each search option individually and perform a search. 2. Combine multiple search options in a single search and verify results.
Use the free-text search field with restaurant name, address, and menu terms.
cau_units.expected_result: Each search option returns relevant results. Combined searches correctly apply all selected criteria simultaneously.
cau_units.actual_result: Price, destination, restaurant type, and dish searches returned correct results. Free-text search returned accurate results for restaurant name and address but did not always match menu item terms.
cau_units.tester_observations: Testers found the free-text search useful but were surprised that searching for a specific dish name in the free-text field did not consistently return the same results as the dedicated dish search option. A change request has been raised; see CR-01 in Section 4.2.

cau_units.title: Search Results - Map View
cau_units.description: Search results should be displayed on a map with restaurant pins, a maximum of one hundred results, a default zoom level, and a filtering menu button.
preconditions: A search has been performed that returns at least one result.
cau_units.expected_result: 1. Perform a search by destination. 2. Observe the map view. 3. Tap a pin and verify the information link is accessible. 4. Tap the filtering button and verify the filtering menu appears. Restaurant locations appear as pins on the map. The user's location is visible. Tapping a pin reveals an information link. The filtering button opens the filtering menu.
cau_units.actual_result: All elements functioned as specified. Map loaded correctly with GPS position shown. Filtering menu was accessible with a single tap.
cau_units.tester_observations: Testers commented positively on the clarity of pin placement. One tester noted that the default zoom level felt slightly too close when there were many nearby restaurants; this has been noted as an enhancement suggestion.

cau_units.title: Search Results - List View
cau_units.description: Search results should be displayed in a list with restaurant name, distance, type of food, average price, a short description, a link to the restaurant website, and an information link. Results should be scrollable and sortable.
preconditions: A search by price has been performed.
cau_units.actual_result: 1. Perform a search by price and verify the list view appears by default. 2. Scroll through the results. 3. Use the sorting header to sort by each available option. 4. Verify each list item includes all required fields. List view displays correctly with all required fields per item. Sorting functions correctly for each option. The list is scrollable when there are more results than fit on screen. List view functioned as specified. All required data fields were present. Sorting by price and distance operated correctly. Sorting by restaurant name was alphabetically correct.
cau_units.tester_observations: No significant issues. Testers requested that the restaurant website link open in a browser rather than within the application. This has been raised as CR-02 in Section 4.2. FR19 (Sorting Results) coverage confirmed in this test case.

linked_requirements.title: Mobile application - Search result in a list view
Usage of the result in the list view

linked_crus.action:  Search results can be viewed in a list.

Each element should include the restaurant name, telephone number, type of food, distance according to the user’s position, average price, a short two-line description, a link to the restaurant’s web-page and an information link.
 There should be maximally 100 results displayed.
If the result contains more restaurants than what can be displayed on the screen at one time, the user should be able to scroll through them.
 When searching by price the restaurants should be sorted according to the following order: 1.
specific dish  When searching by a search option, other than price, the restaurants should be sorted according to the following order: 1.
specific dish  The list view should include a header with different selectable sorting options.
 The list view should include a button that, when selected, should display different filtering options in a filtering menu.
When viewing the results in a list, a user should be able to sort the results according to price, distance, restaurant type, specific dish or restaurant name.
 When sorting by restaurant name, specific dish or restaurant type the results should be ordered alphabetically.
 When sorting by price the results should be ordered from cheapest to most expensive.
 When sorting by distance the results should be ordered from closets to furthest distance according to the user’s position.
The results displayed in the list view should be user friendly and easy to understand.
Selecting an element in the result list should only take one click.

cau_units.description: If the system loses the connection to the Internet or to the GPS device, or if the system receives unexpected input, the user should be informed.
preconditions: The mobile application is running and the user is logged in.

cau_units.tester_observations: Testers confirmed that error notifications were clear and allowed them to understand the cause of the interruption. Recovery after restoring connectivity was smooth and required no restart of the application.

cau_units.title: Download of Updated Application Releases
cau_units.actual_result: When an updated version of the mobile application is published to the application store, the user should be able to download and install the update through the same application store mechanism used for the initial installation, at no cost. The user has the Amazing Lunch Indicator installed. An updated version of the application has been published to the supported application store. 1. Open the application store on the mobile device. 2. Navigate to the Amazing Lunch Indicator listing or the updates section. 3. Initiate the update download. 4. Verify the update installs without error. 5. Launch the updated application and confirm the version number reflects the new release. 6. Verify that existing user credentials and preferences are preserved following the update. The updated application is available in the application store at no charge. The update installs without error. The user is not required to re-register. Previously stored login credentials and language preferences are retained. NOT TESTED in v1.1. No test environment simulating an application store update was available during the original UAT sessions. This test case is added in v1.2 and must be executed against the next application release.

cau_units.title: Behaviour When Search Returns No Results
cau_units.description: When a search query returns no matching restaurants, the application should inform the user clearly that no results were found and provide guidance on how to modify the search criteria.
preconditions: The user is logged in. The device has an active Internet and GPS connection.
cau_units.expected_result: 1. Perform a search using a combination of criteria that is known to return no results (e.g. a highly specific dish name in a location where no matching restaurant exists). 2. Observe the application response in both map view and list view. 3. Verify that an appropriate message is displayed to the user. 4. Verify that the application does not crash or display an unformatted empty state. 5. Confirm that the user is able to return to the search page and modify criteria without restarting the application. The application displays a clear, user-friendly message indicating that no restaurants matched the search criteria. Both map and list views handle the empty state gracefully without errors. The user is presented with an option or guidance to modify the search. The application remains fully functional after an empty result.
cau_units.actual_result: NOT TESTED in v1.1. The original UAT sessions did not include a scenario specifically designed to produce an empty result set. This test case is added in v1.2 and must be executed and signed off before the next release.
cau_units.tester_observations: No tester observations available. The project team should prepare a test dataset that guarantees an empty result for at least one search combination. The empty state message, if present, should be reviewed for clarity and consistency with the application's language selection setting.

cau_units.description: An administrator should be able to select a preferred language for the web-portal from Swedish, English, Spanish, and French.
preconditions: The administrator is logged in to the web-portal.
cau_units.actual_result: 1. Locate the language selection control on the web-portal. 2. Select each of the four available languages in turn: English, Swedish, French, and Spanish. 3. cau_units.tester_observations: No issues were reported. Administrators noted that the language selection behaviour was consistent with that available to restaurant owners, which ensured a familiar experience across both stakeholder groups.

cau_units.title: System Availability
cau_units.description: The average system availability, excluding network failures, should exceed ninety-eight percent of the time.
cau_units.actual_result: The system is deployed in a test environment that replicates the intended production configuration. Monitoring tooling is in place to record uptime and downtime events. 1. Monitor system availability continuously across a simulated usage period of one thousand hours. 2. Record all instances of system unavailability that are attributable to the application rather than to network or GPS infrastructure. 3. Calculate the overall availability percentage. System availability exceeds ninety-eight percent across the one thousand hour measurement period. The planned target is above ninety-nine percent, with a desired target of one hundred percent. System availability measured at 99.2 percent across the observation period, satisfying both the MUST and PLAN thresholds. The two downtime events recorded were attributable to scheduled maintenance windows.
cau_units.tester_observations: No unexpected outages were observed. The team noted that the scheduled maintenance events should be communicated to users in advance through an in-application notification.
